measurement: SG aim-circle, core, and boss-silhouette screen geometry, extracted from owner-drawn overlays on full-resolution frames (2026-07-22)
status: HYPOTHESIS-SUPPORTING MEASUREMENT - the model form it implies (screen-fixed aim circle + screen-fixed core hitbox + edge-weighted pellet ring + world-scaled silhouette) passes a no-free-parameter landing check against all 8 landing cells of soda-tb-sg-core-hr-windows.json; formal 16-cell fit PENDING; NOTHING ENACTED.

method.summary: The owner draws overlays on native-resolution (2622x1206) screenshots (magenta outline = boss silhouette, cyan circle = core / grey aim circle); the session extracts them by color-threshold, flood-fills the silhouette, and circle-fits the rings. This is the method of record for boss/circle geometry: direct segmentation of the un-annotated frames was tried earlier and is INFEASIBLE (dark mech on grey fog).
method.semantics: Owner: 'everything inside the pink line is the boss (not including the line)'; 'inside and not including the blue circle' for core and grey-circle tracings. Radii therefore use the stroke's INNER edge (5th-percentile radial distance); ring-fit pen slop is ~6 px.
method.script: docs/probes/drawn-geometry/extract_drawn_geometry.py (cv2; debug overlays verified visually before any number was trusted)

inputs.role: clean frame, near band (clock 02:16), boss in owner-chosen near-neutral pose
SAME frame + magenta boss outline + cyan core circle
brid-silent-track (SG) solo, near band (clock 02:06): cyan tracing of the grey aim circle at Hit Rate 0
soda-twinkling-bunny (SG) control fight, near band in a Full Burst window (clock 02:14): cyan tracing of the grey aim circle at Hit Rate 38.91

ownerRulingsThisSession: The 8-cell band assignments of soda-tb-sg-core-hr-windows.json are CONFIRMED correct (owner hand-measured) - candidate (d) band-misassignment is DEAD.
Boss on-screen size at a given band is the same across recordings at the same resolution; only limb animation varies, and the traced frames were chosen near a neutral pose.

Pellet impacts are visually weighted toward the OUTER EDGE of the grey circle, arriving in groups of 2-3 along the rim - not center-clustered (owner observation from footage; independently supported quantitatively by the core rates below).

Boss shape: four legs, two large shoulders/arms, narrow hourglass waist, core at the waist center - so as the boss shrinks with range, MORE wide hittable structure enters the fixed circle, holding coverage roughly flat.

core_px.note: diameter ~32 px matches the older near-band '31 px' core figure, implying the old 31->17 px core-size series were DIAMETERS

silhouette_near.note: flood-filled from the outline incl. pen-crossing pockets (~5k px)

derived.circleShrinkAtHr3891: 79.3 -> 48.2 px = 39% shrink, weapon-matched (both SG). The live cone's SG sigma-shrink (0.009/HR => 35% at 38.91) is essentially the CIRCLE shrink applied to the wrong object; the earlier '9-25% measured' figure was an artifact of the now-dead centered-Gaussian premise.

landingCheck_noFreeParameters.model: landing = coverage of the HR-state circle by the traced silhouette scaled per band by px ~ 1/distance (band distances 20.7/30.7/40.7/50.7 from the effective-range readout), circle centered on the core
landingCheck_noFreeParameters.verdict: 6 of 8 cells within ~0.03 with ZERO fitted parameters; the 'impossibly flat' landing column is reproduced. The near band over-predicts in both arms (candidates: loose outline, aim wander, popup dropout).

derived.coreCheck: With radial pellet density p(r) ~ r^k inside the circle: k=1 (uniform) over-predicts near-HR39 core 2-3x; k~2 gives 0.037-0.046 vs pooled measured 0.050, and at HR0 predicts 0.008 vs measured 0.008. The four ON-arm core cells (.074/.029/.079/.022, 3-11 cores each) are statistically consistent with a FLAT ~0.05 - i.e. the core HITBOX may be screen-fixed like the circle, with only the silhouette scaling. Formal fit pending.
derived.resolvedPuzzles: This dissolves BOTH open anomalies of the 2026-07-22 SG geometry handoff section 6: the flat landing column (non-convex silhouette keeps filling the fixed circle) and the non-monotone mid-cell core rate (binomial noise around a flat screen-space core rate).

openItems: Formal 16-cell likelihood fit (ring exponent k, screen-fixed vs scaling core hitbox, landing efficiency factor) with binomial CIs - pending.
Nice-to-have strengthener: trace soda-twinkling-bunny's own grey circle in an HR-off gap of the same fight -> same-unit same-weapon HR contrast (current contrast is weapon-matched but cross-unit).
Near-band over-prediction (0.86 vs 0.78 / 1.00 vs 0.93) unexplained - loose outline vs aim wander vs popup dropout not yet separated.
Burst 2 near-ON window (video 47-62) recount stays open as a pure reproducibility backstop.
Any engine change from this is a gated /scientific-method pass - not this session.
